result.text: Santa Clara 76-71 Saint Mary's (Mar 9, 2026) Game Recap - ESPN Skip to main content Skip to navigation ESPN NFL NFL NBA NBA NCAAM NCAAM NCAAW NCAAW MLB MLB NHL NHL Soccer Soccer More Sports More Sports Watch Watch Fantasy Fantasy Where to Watch Search Saint Mary's Gaels vs Santa Clara Broncos West Coast Conference Tournament - Semifinal Santa Clara Broncos Santa Clara SCU 26-7 , 15-3 WCC 76 26-7 , 15-3 WCC Final 1 2 T Santa Clara SCU 32 44 76 Saint Mary's SMC 35 36 71 21 Saint Mary's Gaels Saint Mary's SMC 27-5 , 16-2 WCC 71 27-5 , 16-2 WCC Gamecast Recap Box Score Play-by-Play Team Stats Videos Santa Clara beats No. 21 Saint Mary's 76-71, advances to WCC title game vs Gonzaga By AP Mar 10, 2026, 02:07 am Facebook Facebook Messenger Twitter Email LAS VEGAS -- — Sash Gavalyugov scored 23 points including a back-breaking 3-pointer to lead Santa Clara to a 76-71 victory over No. 21 Saint Mary's in the semifinals of the West Coast Conference Tournament on Monday night to take a big step toward making the Big Dance. The Broncos (26-7) will play No. 12 Gonzaga (29-3) in the championship game on Tuesday night. The winner receives the conference's automatic bid to the NCAA Tournament. While the Bulldogs are safely in the tournament, Santa Clara was considered on the bubble. The Broncos entered this game No. 42 in the NCAA's NET rankings and No. 37 in Kenpom. Saint Mary's (27-5) could take a hit in the tournament seeding, but the Gaels figure to get into the field of 68. They were No. 20 in the NET and No. 22 in KenPom. This is the first time since 2021 that the WCC final hasn't been between Gonzaga and Saint Mary's. Santa Clara coach Herb Sendek claimed his 600th career victory, a somewhat mild upset over the Gaels, who were favored by 5 1/2 points at Sportsbook. Elijah Mahi added 19 points for the Broncos and Allen Graves finished with 10. Paulius Murauskas scored 26 points to lead Saint Mary's, Mikey Lewis had 23 and Andrew McKeever totaled 11 points and 11 rebounds. There were 14 lead changes and six ties, and the largest advantage by either team was seven points. The Broncos were clinging to a two-point lead in the closing seconds, and Gavalyugov drained a 3-pointer with 12.1 seconds left to beat the shot clock. result.text: 10-seed Texas A&M will face 5-seed Saint Mary's in March Madness Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Recruiting SEC Sports Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports Texas A&M's March Madness seed, opponent, destination revealed Dylan Flippo Aggies Wire After a 21-11 (11-7 in SEC play) campaign, Texas A&M basketball's seeding and opponent for the 2026 NCAA Tournament have officially been revealed. On Selection Sunday, the Aggies were announced as the No. 10 seed in the South Region and will face No. 7 seed Saint Mary's in the first round of March Madness. Texas A&M will travel to Oklahoma City, Oklahoma, to take on the Gaels, marking the sixth time the two programs have faced each other. The last time the Aggies faced Saint Mary's was in 2010 in Bryan-College Station, in which the Maroon and White secured a 70-53 win. Texas A&M's entry into the postseason marks the fourth consecutive appearance in the NCAA Tournament. In Bucky McMillan's first year at the helm in Bryan-College Station, the Aggies will put on their dancing shoes and compete against the Gaels on Thursday, March 19, in OKC. While Texas A&M's trip to Nashville, Tennessee, for the SEC Tournament was short-lived, 'Bucky Ball' made a statement in its first appearance against conference competition this season. The high-paced and high-volume shooting style of basketball that McMillan coaches proved to be difficult to stop against elite competition throughout the year. Entering March Madness, the Aggies rank as the No. 11 scoring offense in the country, averaging 87.7 points, while also ranking No. 15 with 10.8 made 3-pointers per contest. The massive weakness with Texas A&M has been its ability to establish physicality inside the paint, outside of graduate forward Rashaun Agee, who set the program record for most double-doubles in a season with 13. Agee has been the heartbeat of the starting lineup for the Maroon and White, but they can't continue to rely on him alone to carry the weight inside. If Texas A&M makes it past Saint Mary's in the first round of the NCAA Tournament, the Aggies would face the winner of No. 2 seed Houston and No. 15 seed Idaho. The Broncos (26-7) will play No. 12 Gonzaga (29-3) in the championship game on Tuesday night. The winner receives the conference's automatic bid to the NCAA Tournament. The Broncos were clinging to a two-point lead in the closing seconds, and Gavalyugov drained a 3-pointer with 12.1 seconds left to beat the shot clock. 10-seed Texas A&M will face 5-seed Saint Mary's in March Madness Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Recruiting SEC Sports Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports Texas A&M's March Madness seed, opponent, destination revealed Dylan Flippo Aggies Wire After a 21-11 (11-7 in SEC play) campaign, Texas A&M basketball's seeding and opponent for the 2026 NCAA Tournament have officially been revealed. On Selection Sunday, the Aggies were announced as the No. 10 seed in the South Region and will face No. 7 seed Saint Mary's in the first round of March Madness. Texas A&M will travel to Oklahoma City, Oklahoma, to take on the Gaels, marking the sixth time the two programs have faced each other. The last time the Aggies faced Saint Mary's was in 2010 in Bryan-College Station, in which the Maroon and White secured a 70-53 win. Texas A&M's entry into the postseason marks the fourth consecutive appearance in the NCAA Tournament. In Bucky McMillan's first year at the helm in Bryan-College Station, the Aggies will put on their dancing shoes and compete against the Gaels on Thursday, March 19, in OKC. While Texas A&M's trip to Nashville, Tennessee, for the SEC Tournament was short-lived, 'Bucky Ball' made a statement in its first appearance against conference competition this season. The high-paced and high-volume shooting style of basketball that McMillan coaches proved to be difficult to stop against elite competition throughout the year. Entering March Madness, the Aggies rank as the No. 11 scoring offense in the country, averaging 87.7 points, while also ranking No. 15 with 10.8 made 3-pointers per contest. The massive weakness with Texas A&M has been its ability to establish physicality inside the paint, outside of graduate forward Rashaun Agee, who set the program record for most double-doubles in a season with 13. Agee has been the heartbeat of the starting lineup for the Maroon and White, but they can't continue to rely on him alone to carry the weight inside. If Texas A&M makes it past Saint Mary's in the first round of the NCAA Tournament, the Aggies would face the winner of No. 2 seed Houston and No. 15 seed Idaho.
